headlines.title: Hezbollah denounces Israel-Lebanon ceasefire deal as a ‘farce’
headlines.summary: “We have given no commitment to anyone,” the Iranian-backed militant group's leader said after Israel and Lebanon announced a new U.S.-mediated agreement.

headlines.title: Hungary lifts block on Ukraine’s E.U. bid, but long timeline could put Kyiv at risk
headlines.summary: Hungary agreed to drop its opposition to opening the formal access talks but is still opposed to the fast-track membership process that Ukraine says it needs as protection from Russia.

headlines.title: Despite censorship, young Chinese are learning the truth about Tiananmen Square
headlines.summary: Authorities have been largely successful at erasing the massacre of protesters who fought for democratic reforms, but the facts are emerging in often unexpected ways.

headlines.title: U.S. and intelligence allies issue rare joint warning about China
headlines.summary: Nations in the Five Eyes intelligence partnership warned that fake profiles and job offers are targeting military officers, spies, and others with access to classified or sensitive information.

headlines.title: Iranian attack leaves 1 dead, dozens injured in Kuwait
headlines.summary: With peace talks stalled, the U.S. and Iran traded strikes in one of the most intense bouts since the increasingly tenuous ceasefire between the two countries began in April.

headlines.title: Why Mogadishu clashes are deepening Somalia’s political crisis again
headlines.summary: Tensions over Somali elections escalate into violence, leaving Mogadishu residents grappling with fear and disruption.

headlines.title: Israel pounds Lebanon despite ceasefire agreement, death toll hits 3,526
headlines.summary: Israel's supreme court annuls government ban on international Red Cross visiting Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails.

headlines.title: Democrat fails to block US measure to deepen Israel military cooperation
headlines.summary: Congressman Ro Khanna says the integration push only boosts Netanyahu, but his push to revoke the measure fell short.

headlines.title: Democrats force vote on Trump’s $1.8bn settlement fund in ‘vote-a-rama’
headlines.summary: Republicans seek to pass $70bn immigration-enforcement funding, but must endure series of votes on controversial issues.

headlines.title: US defence secretary compares Bolivia protests to government ‘overthrow’
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has supported Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz after his electoral victory over left-wing MAS.

headlines.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
headlines.summary: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

headlines.title: Republicans' sweeping election overhaul fails in the Senate
headlines.summary: The SAVE America Act, a far-reaching Republican election overhaul that President Trump said should be his congressional allies' top priority, has failed in the Senate.

headlines.title: Weakened public health powers raise outbreak risks
headlines.summary: Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.

headlines.title: NTSB says United jet was too slow and too low in Newark landing accident
headlines.summary: Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.

headlines.title: New York City reshapes mass transit system to handle World Cup, NBA finals crowds
headlines.summary: New York transit officials are preparing to handle up to 100,000 extra travelers a day as fans arrive in New York and New Jersey for FIFA World Cup matches.

headlines.title: Thongloun revisits his alma mater in Beijing
headlines.summary: When Lao top leader Thongloun Sisoulith stepped into the dormitory where he had lived in 2000, he found everything almost exactly as he remembered — the same layout and the same atmosphere, unchanged even after so many years.

headlines.title: China, Australia bask in solar panel success
headlines.summary: Electrician James Moore, who installed solar panels on the roof of his Sydney home two years ago, said the green energy move has helped him halve his household power bills.

headlines.title: LVMH growing with the world's fastest-evolving consumer market
headlines.summary: Company connects cultures and societies with retail and commerce to develop understanding and trust in an increasingly complex world.

headlines.title: Dishing up photo success
headlines.summary: Chinese photographers are among the winners at the World Food Photography Awards 2026. The photo competition this year received nearly 9,000 entries from over 50 countries. A total of 12 Chinese entries made their respective finals, including one category winner. (Script editor: Julian Shea, Video editor: Brian Chang)

headlines.title: Chinese car brands make deep inroads into Spain
headlines.summary: BARCELONA — Chinese car brands expanded their presence in Spain's automotive market in the first four months of 2026, accounting for 13.7 percent of all new vehicle registrations, according to official industry data.

headlines.title: Kuki organisation in Manipur opposes SIR of electoral rolls
headlines.summary: Kuki Inpi Manipur opposes electoral roll revision, citing exclusion of 59,000 displaced Kuki-Zo individuals affecting democratic representation.

headlines.title: Kharge and Praveen Chakravarty among Congress Rajya Sabha nominees
headlines.summary: Congress announces seven nominees for Rajya Sabha elections, including Mallikarjun Kharge and Pawan Khera, across five states.

headlines.title: Putin calls India reliable partner, hits out at Western interference
headlines.summary: Putin praises India as a reliable ally, asserting its independent foreign policy amidst Western interference in Russia-India relations.

headlines.title: DMK to boycott June 8 INDIA bloc meeting over Congress ‘betrayal’
headlines.summary: DMK will boycott the June 8 INDIA bloc meeting, citing Congress's betrayal after recent Tamil Nadu Assembly elections.

headlines.title: PM Modi good friend, India and U.S. will get to trade deal: Trump
headlines.summary: Trump praises Modi as a good friend, expressing optimism for a forthcoming U.S.-India trade deal amid ongoing negotiations.

headlines.title: Stocks drop as AI rally pauses, US-Iran peace talks stall
headlines.summary: Reuters: Stocks drop as AI rally pauses, US-Iran peace talks stall.

headlines.title: US House backs Russia sanctions, Ukraine aid, in latest blow to Trump
headlines.summary: Reuters: US House backs Russia sanctions, Ukraine aid, in latest blow to Trump.

headlines.title: Leftist Sanchez gains traction ahead of Peru runoff vote, Ipsos poll shows
headlines.summary: Reuters: Leftist Sanchez gains traction ahead of Peru runoff vote, Ipsos poll shows.

headlines.title: Kennedy Center to remove Trump name after court decision
headlines.summary: Reuters: Kennedy Center to remove Trump name after court decision.

headlines.title: Yen hits key 160 level for third session, dollar buoyed by Gulf woes
headlines.summary: Reuters: Yen hits key 160 level for third session, dollar buoyed by Gulf woes.

hackerNews.title: Do transformers need three projections? Systematic study of QKV variants
hackerNews.summary: Transformers have become the standard solution for various AI tasks, with the query, key, and value (QKV) attention formulation playing a central role. However, the individual contribution of these three projections and the impact of omitting some remain poorly understood. We systematically evaluate three projection sharing constraints: a) Q-K=V (shared key-value), b) Q=K-V (shared query-key), and c) Q=K=V (single projection). The last two variants produce symmetric attention maps; to address this, we also explore asymmetric attention via 2D positional encodings. Through experiments spanning synthetic tasks, vision (MNIST, CIFAR, TinyImageNet, anomaly), and language modeling (300M and 1.2B parameter models on 10B tokens), we discovered that our transformers perform on par or occasionally better than the QKV transformer. In language modeling, Q-K=V projection sharing achieves 50% KV cache reduction with only 3.1% perplexity degradation. Crucially, projection sharing is complementary to head sharing (GQA/MQA): combining Q-K=V with GQA-4 yields 87.5% cache reduction, while Q-K=V + MQA achieves 96.9%, enabling practical on-device inference. We show that Q-K=V preserves quality because keys and values can occupy similar representational spaces and attention operates in a low-rank regime, whereas Q=K-V breaks attention directionality. Our results systematically characterize projection sharing as an underexplored instance of weight tying in attention, with direct, quantifiable inference memory benefits, particularly valuable for edge deployment. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/Brainchip-Inc/Do-Transformers-Need-3-Projections

hackerNews.summary: Skills for threat modeling, scanning, triage, patching, plus an autonomous scanning harness you can /customize - anthropics/defending-code-reference-harness

hackerNews.summary: Battle-tested at Alibaba's scale. Hybrid architecture code review tool: deterministic pipelines + LLM Agent, precise line-level comments, built-in fine-tuned ruleset (NPE, thread-safety, XSS, S...

hackerNews.title: VoidZero Is Joining Cloudflare
hackerNews.summary: VoidZero, the team behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, Oxc, and Vite+, is joining Cloudflare. Vite stays open source, vendor-agnostic, and built for everyone.

hackerNews.title: When AI Builds Itself: Our progress toward recursive self-improvement
hackerNews.summary: Our progress toward recursive self-improvement, and its implications.

hackerNews.title: Queen bees emerge from special wax chambers
hackerNews.summary: Royal wax, as well as royal jelly, determines the fate of a honeybee larva

hackerNews.title: South Korean Forums Will Need to Scan Every Images with AI Censorship Tools
hackerNews.summary: Due to recent regulation changes (전기통신사업법), the South Korean government is requiring internet communities and forum owners to scan every user uploaded images and videos on their website, by AI. The hardware to run these AI models are also not provided by government, website owners have to buy datacenter grade Nvidia GPUs by themselves, putting financial pressure to small businesses and forums. Websites will need to implement these hardware and software features, starting immediately from July ...

hackerNews.title: KVarN: Native vLLM backend for KV-cache quantization by Huawei
hackerNews.summary: KVarN is a native vLLM KV-cache quantization backend for your agents: 3-5x more context, throughput above FP16, and FP16-level accuracy. Calibration-free, one flag. - huawei-csl/KVarN

summarySections.title: News and markets
sentences: Global headlines: Hezbollah denounces Israel-Lebanon ceasefire deal as a ‘farce’ (“We have given no commitment to anyone,” the Iranian-backed militant group's leader said after Israel and Lebanon announced a new U.S.-mediated agreement); Hungary lifts block on Ukraine’s E.U. bid, but long timeline could put Kyiv at risk (Hungary agreed to drop its opposition to opening the formal access talks but is still opposed to the fast-track membership process that Ukraine says it needs as protection from Russia); and Despite censorship, young Chinese are learning the truth about Tiananmen Square (Authorities have been largely successful at erasing the massacre of protesters who fought for democratic reforms, but the facts are emerging in often unexpected ways).
Markets leaned lower, with 4 of 7 tracked indexes down; Nikkei 225 down 1.20% was the largest move.
Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes.
